description_long: This is a commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily white, featuring blue and black accent stripes and the 'AIR FRANCE' logo along the side. The aircraft has a low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and a pair of engines mounted at the rear of the fuselage, just ahead of the tail. The tail section is a T-tail design, with the horizontal stabilizer mounted atop the vertical fin. The cockpit is located at the very front of the fuselage, with a row of windows running along the length of the passenger cabin. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it is likely retractable and currently stowed. The overall design is typical of mid-sized commercial jets used for regional or short-haul flights.
description: A white commercial passenger airplane with blue and black stripes, rear-mounted engines, and a T-tail design, branded with 'AIR FRANCE'. The aircraft features a low, swept-wing configuration and a streamlined fuselage.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, the 'AIR FRANCE' logo, swept-back wings, and rear-mounted engines.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the T-tail, twin engines at the back, and the full wingspan.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other side of the fuselage, wing, and tail, with the logo partially visible.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the overall planform, wing sweep, and the arrangement of the tail and engines.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with moderate dihedral and no visible winglets.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear (not visible, inferred from typical design).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the fuselage.
